result.summary: Wix exposes an HTTP API surface (Wix JavaScript SDK / unified API reference, with REST/GraphQL under it) and an MCP server at https://mcp.wix.com/mcp; auth is via Wix API key plus account/site IDs for account-managed/self-managed use, or Wix OAuth app credentials (app ID, app secret, instance ID) for installed apps, and MCP can also be used with no credential or with API key + account ID.

wix_api_key.label: Wix API key
wix_api_key.setup: Go to the [API Keys Manager](https://manage.wix.com/account/api-keys). As an account owner or co-owner, create a key and grant the narrowest site/account access needed. Copy the key value and keep it private. For site-level calls, also collect the target site ID; for account-level calls, collect the account ID.

wix_oauth_app.label: Wix OAuth app credentials
wix_oauth_app.setup: Create a Wix app and configure OAuth in the app dashboard. Use the app's OAuth credentials to request user consent when your app is installed on a site. Wix notes that 3rd-party Wix apps should use OAuth rather than API keys for API access.

wix_account_id.label: Wix account ID
wix_account_id.setup: Find the account ID in the [API Keys Manager](https://manage.wix.com/account/api-keys). Wix requires this alongside an API key for account-scoped integrations, including MCP setups that authenticate with an API key.

wix_site_id.label: Wix site ID
wix_site_id.setup: Obtain the site ID for the target Wix site. Wix says you can get it from the site's dashboard URL after `/dashboard/`, or by querying sites with the Wix Sites API. Some site-level calls require both the site ID and an API key.

wix_app_id.label: Wix app ID
wix_app_id.setup: Open your app's [OAuth page](https://manage.wix.com/app-selector?title=Select+an+App&primaryButtonText=Select+Site&actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fdev.wix.com%2Fapps%2F%7BappId%7D%2Foauth) in the Wix dashboard and copy the `appId`. Wix uses it together with the app secret and an `instanceId` for OAuth-based API access.

wix_instance_id.label: Wix app instance ID
wix_instance_id.setup: Capture the `instanceId` for the app installed on a site. Wix says you can obtain it from the App Instance Installed webhook, from webhook payloads, from service plugin metadata, or from the app instance query parameter on external pages/iframe extensions.

surfaces.name: Wix JavaScript SDK / unified API reference
